results.editorial:
Harvestars Care Centre's Klang branch operates from H-Residence, a stand-alone condominium block in Kawasan 6, Klang — part of what the group markets as Malaysia's first nursing care home built in condo style. The facility sits within a residential tower format and is designed to accommodate around 200 residents, serving seniors who need anything from independent or assisted living through to full nursing care for stroke recovery, dementia, and palliative cases. Harvestars runs a parallel branch in Puchong, making this a two-location chain with a consistent brand identity built around amenity-rich, hotel-adjacent living.

The website indicates a broad clinical scope: 24-hour care delivered by registered nurses, on-call doctors, physiotherapists, and traditional Chinese medicine practitioners. The care menu listed covers stroke rehabilitation, dementia support, palliative care, and assisted daily living. Recreational infrastructure is notably extensive for a nursing home — karaoke room, mahjong room, snooker room, library, massage room, and an in-house cafeteria, plus a natural-farming area. Four meals per day are provided by a dedicated chef. Wi-Fi, special mattresses, and a full-sized closet are listed as standard inclusions.

Because Harvestars operates inside a private residential high-rise, prospective families should ask specifically which floors or units are in use, how emergency evacuation works, and whether lift access is sufficient for bedridden residents. The group has a Facebook page for the Klang branch where recent activity can be checked, but no public pricing is published — call to discuss rates, which are likely to vary by room type and care level. Licence status and JKM certification should be confirmed before admission, as the condo-format model is novel in Selangor.

facts.care_types: Nursing home, Assisted living, Palliative care, Dementia care, Stroke rehabilitation
facts.red_flags: No public pricing. Novel condo-format model — confirm JKM licence and emergency evacuation provisions.

results.editorial:
Hemei Life Care Center's Klang outlet sits at 10, Jalan Batu Tiga Lama, Kawasan 16, Klang — part of a multi-branch chain that also operates in Cheras, Seri Kembangan, Selayang, Sri Damansara, and Johor Bahru. The group positions itself squarely around short-to-medium-term recovery and step-down care rather than purely long-term placement, with long stay, short stay, day care, and post-operative rehabilitation all listed as core offerings. That makes the Klang branch a candidate not just for families seeking permanent residential care but also for patients who need a supervised recovery environment after surgery or a hospital discharge before returning home.

A third-party aggregator (seniorliving.com.my) lists the Klang branch with a capacity of 200 private rooms and quotes care tiers as follows: independent or assisted living from RM 4,000 per month (standard RM 5,000), wheelchair or dementia care from RM 4,500 (standard RM 5,500), and bedridden or tube-feeding care from RM 5,000 (standard RM 6,000), with a RM 300 add-on for a medical bed. Chargeable extras listed include diapers, underpads, gloves, wet wipes, and wound dressing. The website emphasises hotel-style accommodation, CCTV surveillance, four daily meals, safety handrails, and activity programming to support mental health.

Operating hours shown on the contact page are 8:30 AM to 5:30 PM, which raises a practical question about after-hours access for family visits and whether night-shift nursing coverage is equivalent to daytime. The Klang branch was listed as 'coming soon' on some earlier versions of the Hemei website, so families should confirm it is now fully operational and ask for an updated resident count before committing. Specialist services such as physiotherapy and dementia care, which are available at other Hemei branches, should be verified as active at this specific location.

facts.care_types: Assisted living, Nursing home, Day care, Post-operative rehabilitation, Dementia care
facts.red_flags: Branch may have opened recently — verify current operational status. After-hours staffing model not clearly stated.

results.editorial:
Mona Elder Care's Pandan Perdana branch is located at No. 32, Jalan Perdana 1/4, Pandan Perdana, in the part of the district that straddles the KL-Selangor boundary near Cheras. It is one of at least four Mona Elder Care locations in the Klang Valley — the others being in Ampang, Setapak, and Taiping — and the group's website presents a consistent clinical identity across all sites. The branch targets seniors who need nursing home-level care in a residential neighbourhood setting, with particular emphasis on stroke and coma cases, dementia, and palliative care alongside standard elderly nursing care for immobile or medically complex residents.

The services page lists a notably detailed clinical menu: bedsore dressing, nasogastric tube changes, colostomy bag management, Foley catheter care, tracheostomy care, and post-operative support alongside physiotherapy, respite care, day care, and handicapped care for adults with disabilities. This breadth suggests the facility can take on residents who require active nursing intervention, not just assisted daily living. A 24/7 care model is stated across the group's online presence, and the address is described as accessible to nearby clinics, hospitals, and public transport — useful for families attending regular follow-up appointments.

Bed count, specific staff ratios, and pricing are not disclosed publicly by Mona Elder Care; all of these should be asked directly when you call the Pandan Perdana number (+60 14-688 7358). There is no mention of JKM licence numbers, religious affiliation, or the primary languages spoken by caregivers on the website — the latter is relevant for Chinese-speaking families given the Cheras/Pandan Perdana demographic. The Facebook page for the group shows activity but does not distinguish between branches, so clarify which site photos and updates refer to.

facts.care_types: Nursing home, Stroke and coma care, Dementia care, Palliative care, Respite care, Day care, Physiotherapy, Handicapped care
facts.red_flags: No public pricing or bed count. Licence number not published. Confirm which language caregivers speak for non-Malay-speaking residents.

results.editorial:
Jia Yuan Retirement Care Centre is located at Jalan PP1/6, Taman Putra Prima, Puchong — a residential township in the southwest Klang Valley that sits roughly equidistant between Puchong town and Putrajaya. The facility is listed in Google Maps as a retirement home and has a registered company number of 202401017567, suggesting incorporation in 2024. With a 4.1-star rating from 17 Google reviewers, it is one of the smaller-volume reviewed facilities in this batch, and publicly available information about its operations, staffing, and clinical scope is very limited.

Based on its listing category — retirement home rather than nursing home — Jia Yuan likely caters primarily to ambulant or semi-ambulant seniors who need social care and a supervised living environment rather than intensive medical nursing. Taman Putra Prima is a landed residential area, which typically means care homes operating there are shophouse or bungalow conversions rather than purpose-built facilities. No website, Facebook page, or brochure material was identified during research, so there is no way to verify care types, bed count, staffing credentials, or pricing independently from a third-party source.

Families considering Jia Yuan should contact the listed number (+60 11-5455 2599) and ask to visit in person before any decision. Key questions include whether the facility holds a current JKM licence, how many beds are available, what medical oversight exists (on-call doctor or nurse on duty), what happens if a resident's condition deteriorates, and whether the location is equipped for wheelchair or bedridden residents given the likely residential building format. The 2024 registration date means the facility is relatively new, which is worth factoring into your evaluation.

facts.red_flags: No website or social media found. Newly registered (2024). Very limited public information — physical visit essential before committing.

results.editorial:
Pearl Care Elderly Center operates from Seksyen 12, Petaling Jaya — one of PJ's established residential sections near Jalan Kemajuan — and is part of a growing multi-branch network with additional locations in Sections 5, 11, 12A, and Sunway, all within Petaling Jaya. The facility is registered as a Sdn. Bhd. incorporated in August 2020 with registration number 1379500H, and the Google Maps listing categorises it as both a nursing home and a retirement home, indicating a mixed resident profile spanning independent seniors through to high-dependency cases. A 4.9-star rating from 16 reviewers is consistently positive.

The website states that Pearl Care takes residents with severe illness including stroke, coma, tube feeding, and terminal diagnoses such as cancer — placing it firmly in the skilled nursing home category rather than pure assisted living. Clinical services include wound care, medication management, catheterisation, respiratory support, physiotherapy, and assisted daily living. A doctor on-call model is cited, and 24-hour nursing by certified nurses is stated. Three main meals plus two snacks are provided daily, along with laundry, housekeeping, an emergency call system in each room, and a 24-hour mobility vehicle service for medical transport. Pricing is advertised as starting from RM 1,500 per month, which is relatively accessible for PJ; exact rates by room type and care level should be confirmed by phone.

The Seksyen 12 office number — +60 3-7890 2552 — is the direct contact for this branch, with the group hotline at +60 12-525 5594 for general enquiries. Visiting hours operate between 8:30 AM and 7:30 PM. The website supports Chinese (Simplified and Traditional), English, and Malay, suggesting a multilingual-friendly service, though specific caregiver language competencies should be asked about directly. JKM licence number is not published online — request it during your visit.

facts.care_types: Nursing home, Retirement home, Stroke and coma care, Palliative care, Dementia care, Wound care
facts.languages: English, Malay, Chinese (inferred from multilingual website)
facts.red_flags: JKM licence number not published. Confirm exact pricing by care level — advertised RM1,500 starting rate may reflect basic independent living only.

results.editorial:
Pusat Jagaan Warga Emas Al-Fattah is a community-founded care centre operating from a rented residential property at No. 9, Jalan TTS 7/1, Taman Tasik Semenyih, 43500 Semenyih — a quiet lakeside housing estate about 45 minutes southeast of Kuala Lumpur. The centre was established in 2018 by Mohammad Azlan Abdul Rahman and grew from a grass-roots welfare initiative aimed at elderly individuals who lack family support or who are managing chronic conditions such as hypertension, diabetes, or mobility impairment. The Arabic name, its Facebook page identity as 'Villa Al-Fattah,' and its programming all point clearly to an Islamic-oriented facility.

The centre is also registered as Pertubuhan Kebajikan Warga Emas Al-Fattah — a welfare association (pertubuhan) rather than a private care home company — and has accepted public donations through sumbangan.com, suggesting a charitable funding model that likely keeps costs below market rates. Care activities listed include daily health monitoring, medication management, wound treatment, light exercise, religious programmes (congregational prayers and classes), nutritious meals adapted to dietary restrictions, and recreational activities. The residential setting means rooms and shared spaces are home-like rather than clinical, which suits ambulant or lightly dependent seniors but may be less suited to high-acuity cases requiring intensive nursing.

Families looking at Al-Fattah should be aware that this is a welfare-oriented, Malay-Muslim setting: residents should be comfortable with Islamic prayer schedules and communal religious observance. Bed count, staffing ratios, and whether a nurse or doctor provides formal medical oversight were not confirmed in available sources — these are essential questions to ask when contacting En. Azlan at 017-992 5503 (the contact listed in third-party sources, though the Google Maps listing shows +60 3-8726 0824). Confirm whether the facility holds a JKM licence, as its welfare-association structure may place it under a different regulatory framework than private care homes.

facts.red_flags: Welfare-association model — confirm JKM licence status. Medical oversight (nurse on duty) not confirmed. Islamic setting unsuitable for non-Muslim residents who are not comfortable with religious observance.

results.editorial:
Elderlove Living Care Centre's Puchong branch occupies ground-floor shophouse units (SS-G-23, SS-G-23A, and SS-G-25) in Skypod Square on Persiaran Puchong Jaya Selatan, Bandar Puchong Jaya — a commercial podium in one of Puchong's newer mixed-use developments with easy carpark access and proximity to major roads. This is one of several Elderlove branches across the Klang Valley (others include Sungai Long and Kajang), and unusually for the sector, the group was co-founded by two medical specialists — a physician and a neurologist — supported by a team that includes a geriatrician, nephrologist, physiotherapists, occupational therapists, and a dietitian. That clinical founding team is reflected in the care scope and in the dementia and neurological care programme specifically.

Elderlovemissi.com documents pricing for Puchong as ranging from approximately RM 3,400 to RM 4,500 per month for long-term care depending on room occupancy, with single beds at the top and five-to-six-bedded rooms at the lower end; day care runs at RM 110–135 per day. The package includes three main meals, whole-day snacks, laundry, weekly physiotherapy, monthly specialist review, yearly blood test, monthly grooming, and a monthly vitamin infusion — a notably inclusive bundle compared to facilities that charge many of these as extras. Rooms come in two-bedded, four-bedded, and six-bedded configurations with air-conditioning, safety handrails, ramps, non-slip flooring, and emergency call systems.

All nursing staff are described as registered Malaysian nurses with SKM Level 3 certification and CPR certification; an AI fitness centre, cooking and baking sessions, crafts, board games, and health talks round out the activities programme. Families considering this branch for a cognitively impaired relative should ask specifically whether the dementia programme run by the Geriatrician and Neurologist is active at the Puchong site, or whether residents need to attend another branch for specialist sessions. No religious affiliation is stated. The 5-star Google rating from 15 reviews is a small sample — ask for family references.

facts.care_types: Nursing home, Assisted living, Dementia and memory care, Physiotherapy, Day care, Post-stroke care
facts.red_flags: Confirm whether specialist dementia/neurology programme is run at Puchong branch specifically. Small review sample (15). Ask for family references.

results.editorial:
Oasis Nursing Home — known in Chinese as 安康之家疗养院 — operates from a three-storey semi-detached house with a lift at 6, Taman Pegaga U12/1AA, 40170 Shah Alam, in the U12 section of Shah Alam's newer northwestern townships. The Chinese name and the U12 location (near the Setia Alam and Elmina growth corridors) suggest the facility primarily serves the Chinese-speaking community in that part of Shah Alam and Klang. A 5-star Google rating drawn from 15 reviews is encouraging, but a 15-review sample is small and should be weighed accordingly. The facility has a Facebook page (oasisnursingcare123) and a basic Wix website, but neither source provided detailed service or pricing information during research.

The Google Maps and Wix website describe the facility as a professional nursing care centre managed by a team of doctor, nurses, and caregivers, indicating at least some level of formal medical oversight. The semi-detached residential format with a built-in lift makes it feasible for wheelchair users and residents with limited mobility, which is an important structural consideration in converted residential properties. The three-storey configuration with a lift is a practical advantage over single-storey shophouse conversions. No detailed information on care scope — whether the facility takes dementia, tube-feeding, or palliative cases — was available from public sources.

Families should contact the facility at +60 12-270 3166 and ask about the level of care accepted, nurse-to-resident ratios across day and night shifts, whether a doctor attends regularly, and what the current vacancy and pricing situation is. The Chinese name 安康之家 (literally 'peace, health, and home') is consistent with a Chinese-community-oriented setting, so ask whether Mandarin and Cantonese are spoken by care staff, which is important for residents who are not fluent in English or Malay. Confirm JKM licence details before proceeding.

facts.languages: Likely Mandarin/Cantonese (inferred from Chinese name and location)
facts.red_flags: Very limited public information. Small review sample. Care scope and pricing not publicly disclosed. JKM licence not confirmed.

results.editorial:
Unique Family Care Center is located at 2, Jalan PU 1/10A, Taman Puchong Utama, 47140 Puchong — a landed residential estate in the northeastern part of Puchong, roughly midway between Puchong Jaya and the Bukit Jalil corridor. Google Maps lists it as a nursing home and senior citizen centre, and it carries a 4.9-star rating from 15 reviewers. The 'family' emphasis in the name and the residential address are consistent with a home-like, smaller-scale facility operating out of a converted house or linked-terrace rather than a purpose-built care complex. No website was identified, and the facility's nursinghome.my listing explicitly notes it does not use a computer system.

Publicly available information about the scope of care, staffing, bed count, and pricing is essentially absent. The two contact numbers on record — +60 14-332 7562 and +60 16-202 6751 — are the only confirmed reach points. The senior citizen centre classification alongside nursing home suggests the facility may serve a mix of ambulant day-care residents and those requiring full residential nursing care, which is a common arrangement in smaller community homes in Puchong. The high rating despite the thin online footprint suggests it may rely on word-of-mouth referrals within a close-knit community.

Any family considering Unique Family Care Center should arrange an in-person visit before making any decision. Ask about the total number of beds, the ratio of nursing-trained staff to residents, what conditions are accepted (particularly whether stroke, dementia, tube-feeding, or bedridden cases are managed), what happens medically in an emergency, and whether the facility holds a current JKM licence. The residential setting and small online presence mean independent verification is limited — a face-to-face assessment is especially important here.

facts.red_flags: No website. No computer system per directory listing. Extremely limited public information — in-person visit essential. JKM licence not confirmed.

results.editorial:
Mona Elder Care's Ampang branch is situated at 2, Jalan Sri Kemuning, Lembah Jaya Selatan, 68000 Ampang — a residential pocket just south of the main Ampang town corridor, with reasonable access to Hospital Ampang and the broader Ampang-Cheras clinical network. This is one of at least four Mona Elder Care locations in the Klang Valley, sharing a common brand, clinical identity, and service menu with the Pandan Perdana branch. The group's 24/7 model and its emphasis on medically complex cases — stroke, coma, tube feeding, post-operative recovery, and tracheostomy care — position this as a nursing home that can take on cases hospitals discharge earlier than families might expect.

The services page of monaeldercare.com lists nine primary care types offered across the network: respite care, palliative care, home nursing, stroke and coma care, senior citizens care, handicapped care, physiotherapy, dementia care, and day care. Clinical procedures documented include bedsore dressing, NG tube changes, colostomy bag management, Foley catheter care, tracheostomy care, post-operative care, and cerebral palsy support. This is a more clinically detailed public disclosure than most similarly-sized Selangor nursing homes provide, which is useful for families assessing whether a specific care need can be met. The facility describes a structured 24-hour shift system across morning, afternoon, evening, and night shifts.

With a 4.7-star rating from 15 Google reviewers, this Ampang branch performs slightly better on volume than the Pandan Perdana location's identical 17-review base. As with all Mona Elder Care sites, no pricing, bed count, or staff ratio figures are publicly available — request these by contacting +60 16-616 4535. Language coverage by caregivers is not disclosed; given the Ampang location's mixed Chinese, Malay, and Indian demographic profile, ask directly about Mandarin, Cantonese, and Tamil capability. Confirm the JKM licence number and ask whether the Ampang site has its own registered licence or operates under a group licence.

facts.care_types: Nursing home, Stroke and coma care, Dementia care, Palliative care, Respite care, Physiotherapy, Handicapped care, Day care
facts.red_flags: No public pricing or bed count. Confirm whether Ampang site has its own JKM licence. Ask about caregiver language competency for Chinese- and Tamil-speaking families.
